specs.title: Scale vs. Reasoning Accuracy Simulation
specs.description: Simulate reasoning benchmark accuracy across model sizes (1B–13B params) on tasks of varying difficulty
specs.code:
import random, math

random.seed(42)
sizes = [("Small (1B)", 1), ("Medium (3B)", 3), ("Large (7B)", 7), ("XLarge (13B)", 13)]
tasks = {"Basic Arithmetic": 0.15, "Multi-step Logic": 0.25, "Counterfactual": 0.35}

print(f"{'Model':<14} {'Task':<20} {'Accuracy':>8} {'95% CI':>12}")
print("-"*54)
for name, params in sizes:
    base = 0.45 + 0.05 * math.log2(params + 0.5)
    for task, difficulty in tasks.items():
        acc = min(0.97, base + random.gauss(0, 0.02) - difficulty * 0.12)
        ci = 1.96 * math.sqrt(acc*(1-acc)/200)
        print(f"{name:<14} {task:<20} {acc:>7.1%}  ±{ci:>6.2%}")

metrics.name: large_vs_small_hard_gap
metrics.description: Accuracy advantage of 7B vs 1B on hardest task

specs.title: Fine-tuning Disproportionate Benefit Simulation
specs.description: Simulate zero-shot vs fine-tuned accuracy across model sizes to test if fine-tuning helps smaller models more
specs.code:
import random, math

random.seed(42)
models = [("Small (1B)", 1.0), ("Medium (3B)", 3.0), ("Large (7B)", 7.0), ("XLarge (13B)", 13.0)]
tasks = ["Basic Arithmetic", "Multi-step Logic", "Counterfactual"]

print(f"{'Model':<14} {'Task':<20} {'Zero-shot':>9} {'Fine-tuned':>10} {'Gain':>6}")
print("-" * 61)
for name, params in models:
    base = 0.45 + 0.05 * math.log2(params + 0.5)
    for task in tasks:
        diff = {"Basic Arithmetic": 0.10, "Multi-step Logic": 0.18, "Counterfactual": 0.22}
        task_diff = diff[task]
        ft_boost = 0.20 / math.sqrt(params + 0.5) + random.gauss(0, 0.01)
        zero = min(0.95, base + random.gauss(0, 0.02) - task_diff)
        ft = min(0.97, zero + ft_boost)
        gain = ft - zero
        print(f"{name:<14} {task:<20} {zero:>7.1%}  {ft:>8.1%}  {gain:>+5.1%}")

metrics.name: gain_ratio
metrics.description: Relative gain ratio: small model gain / large model gain
